summary.audit_decision: The phase-2 NAT-rebinding proxy runner is packaged and fail-closed, but AWS credential gates still block live execution.

runner.name: proxy_build
runner.meaning: Proxy is built inside the run artifact.

runner.name: proxy_upstream_bind
runner.meaning: Proxy can bind local upstream sockets for remote NLB targets.

runner.name: chunk_env
runner.meaning: Client can be forced to send after the proxy switch.

runner.name: proxy_rebind_observed
runner.meaning: Summary separates proxy-observed rebind from forwarding echo.

runner.name: phase2_claim_boundary
runner.meaning: Result boundary avoids claiming public active migration API support.

client.name: payload_chunks
client.meaning: Chunked send is opt-in and default-compatible.

client.name: chunk_delay
client.meaning: Chunk delay is opt-in and default-compatible.

client.name: chunk_sender
client.meaning: Payload is split across multiple sends when configured.

client.name: client_result_fields
client.meaning: Client result records the configured chunk count.

proxy.name: remote_wildcard_bind
proxy.meaning: Remote upstream targets can use wildcard local binding.

proxy.name: loopback_preserved
proxy.meaning: Loopback behavior remains stable for local Chrome controls.

proxy.name: bind_recorded
proxy.meaning: Proxy result records the bind decision.

claim_boundary.safe_claim: The repository now has a runnable phase-2 mode that can place a UDP rebinding proxy between the s2n client and AWS NLB, force chunked client traffic after the proxy switch, and classify proxy-observed rebinding separately from forwarding echo.
claim_boundary.unsafe_claim: This audit proves live AWS NLB+s2n NAT-rebinding continuity, application-triggered active migration, browser handover, or current upstream s2n public active migration API support.
claim_boundary.next_step: After AWS identity opens, run forwarding echo first; only if that passes, run this mode with PATH_CHANGE_MODE=rebinding_proxy, PAYLOAD_CHUNKS>1, and CHUNK_DELAY_MS>0.
